name: Los Zumaques Trail
name_original: Fortaleza de la Mota y bosque de las esculturas - circular por el sendero los Zumaques
description: Hike the moderate Los Zumaques Trail, a 5.2-mile loop offering panoramic views of Alcalá la Real and unique rock sculptures.
region_intro: Hiking around Alcalá La Real provides access to diverse landscapes, including vast olive groves, cereal fields, and low scrubland, set within the Sierra Sur de Jaén. The region is characterized by impressive rock formations such as Los Tajos and features several mountain ranges, offering varied terrain for outdoor activities. Trails often integrate historical landmarks like the Fortaleza de la Mota, blending natural exploration with cultural heritage. Best hiking trails around Alcalá La Real The most popular hiking route is Los Zumaques Trail, a 5.2 miles (8.4 km) trail that takes about 2 hours 29 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers panoramic views and is particularly scenic in autumn due to the vibrant sumac trees. Another top favourite among local hikers is Alcalá loop from Alcalá la Real, a moderate 5.2 miles (8.3 km) path. This route traverses the local landscape, often passing through olive groves and open fields. Local hikers also love the View of Alcalá la Real Castle – Church of San Antón loop from Alcalá la Real, a 4.0 miles (6.4 km) trail leading through historical areas with views of the Fortaleza de la Mota, often completed in about 1 hour 46 minutes. Hiking around Alcalá La Real is defined by extensive olive groves, impressive rock formations like Los Tajos, and historical watchtowers. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Alcalá La Real are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 250 reviews. More than 1,400 hikers have used komoot to explore Alcalá La Real's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Alcalá La Real?
A: Alcalá La Real offers a comprehensive network of over 20 hiking trails, catering to various abilities. You'll find a good mix, with 11 easy routes, 10 moderate options, and 1 challenging trail for experienced hikers.

Q: What natural landmarks can I explore while hiking in Alcalá La Real?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can discover impressive rock formations like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3230098" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Tajos&lt;/a&gt;, which host remnants of ancient Mediterranean forests. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983671568/los-zumaques-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Zumaques Trail&lt;/a&gt; is particularly known for its sumac trees, which turn a vivid reddish hue in autumn. You might also encounter the unique, stony tableland of Los Llanos, offering a distinctive 'moon-like' landscape. Don't miss the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4225063" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zurreón Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; for a refreshing sight.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking trails in Alcalá La Real?
A: Yes, Alcalá La Real has several easy routes suitable for families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14622085" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Alcalá la Real Castle – Church of San Antón loop from Alcalá la Real&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 6.4 km trail that takes about 1 hour 46 minutes, offering historical views without being too strenuous. Many of the 11 easy trails available are great for a family outing.

Q: What historical attractions can be seen along the hiking routes?
A: Many trails in Alcalá La Real seamlessly blend natural exploration with cultural heritage. The imposing &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5336873" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fortaleza de la Mota&lt;/a&gt;, a National Monument, dominates the skyline and offers spectacular panoramic views from numerous paths. You can also explore the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3229479" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Marcos Rock Sculpture Forest&lt;/a&gt;, where art is carved directly into natural rock.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, circular routes are a popular option in Alcalá La Real. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983671568/los-zumaques-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Zumaques Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a well-known moderate circular route, approximately 8.4 km long, offering panoramic views. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38232746" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcalá loop from Alcalá la Real&lt;/a&gt;, which is also a moderate circular path traversing olive groves and open fields.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Alcalá La Real?
A: The hiking routes in Alcalá La Real are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average score of 4.5 stars from nearly 300 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the integration of historical landmarks, and the stunning autumnal colors, especially along the Los Zumaques Trail.

Q: When is the best time of year to go hiking in Alcalá La Real?
A: While hiking is enjoyable year-round, autumn is particularly recommended. The region's sumac trees, especially along the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983671568/los-zumaques-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Zumaques Trail&lt;/a&gt;, turn a vibrant reddish hue, painting the landscape and attracting many hikers. Spring also offers pleasant temperatures and blooming flora.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Yes, for those seeking a more demanding experience, Alcalá La Real offers at least one difficult trail. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14622191" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Alcalá la Real Castle – Los Tajos loop from Alcalá la Real&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 17.8 km route that takes around 5 hours to complete, providing extensive views and varied terrain.

Q: What kind of landscapes will I encounter on the trails?
A: Hiking in Alcalá La Real offers a diverse range of landscapes. You'll traverse vast olive groves, cereal fields, and low scrubland. The terrain also features impressive rock formations like Los Tajos, ancient Mediterranean forests, and the unique, stony tableland of Los Llanos. Many routes also follow streams and rivers, highlighting the region's natural heritage.

Q: Can I combine hiking with exploring historical sites in Alcalá La Real?
A: Absolutely. Alcalá La Real is known for seamlessly blending its natural beauty with its rich history. Many trails lead to or past significant landmarks. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14622075" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Antón – Los Tajos loop from Alcalá la Real&lt;/a&gt; offers views of historical areas and natural formations. The region also features a 'Watchtower Route' that takes adventurers past nine defensive towers from the Middle Ages.

Q: Are there any trails that highlight the region's water features?
A: Yes, the region's natural heritage includes several water features. The Rio Velillos Trail (PR-A 414), for instance, highlights the vital role of water in the area, winding through olive groves, cereal fields, and riverside vegetation. You can also find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4225063" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zurreón Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2379252" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the San Juan River&lt;/a&gt; as points of interest.
tips: Also known as the castle of Alcalá la Real, the fortress of La Mota is a defensive enclosure, located on the hill of La Mota at 1029m, in the city of Alcalá la Real, in the province of Jaén. It is dated from the Nasrid period (13th and 14th centuries), although with some previous elements.

It is a small forest with a few sculptures carved in the natural rock of the area. At the top, there is a cave with a statue of a goat "watching over" the city.
To access it (it is not signposted) you have to take a small path between a small booth with the Nativity of San Marcos and a stone wall.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Los Zumaques Trail?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas aren't detailed, the trail is located in Alcalá la Real, Jaén, Spain. You'll likely find parking options within the town or near the trailhead, which often starts from urban areas or accessible points. It's a circular route, so you'll return to your starting point.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for dogs?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Los Zumaques Trail is considered dog-friendly, making it a great option if you want to bring your canine companion along for the hike.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is particularly stunning in autumn. This is when the sumac bushes, from which the trail gets its name, transform into vibrant reddish-orange hues, creating a spectacular display of color.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for the Los Zumaques Trail?
faqs.answer: No, there is no mention of permits or entrance fees required to hike the Los Zumaques Trail. It is generally accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers varied terrain, including ravines, narrow paths, and impressive rocky cliffs known as 'Los Tajos'. You'll pass through extensive olive groves, stone-walled sections, and a humid holm oak forest. There are also unique geological formations like 'Nidos de Avispa' and 'la sillita de la reina', and open areas with stone mounds called 'Los Llanos'.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the trail?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several notable features, including the impressive rocky cliffs of 'Los Tajos' and unique geological formations. The route also offers excellent views of the historic Fortaleza de la Mota (Castle of Alcalá la Real) and leads to the San Marcos Rock Sculpture Forest, where a local artist has carved figures into the rock.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I see while hiking?
faqs.answer: The area is home to various bird species, such as common kestrels, jackdaws, and crows. You might also spot mammals like mountain goats, as well as different reptiles.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Los Zumaques Trail is considered suitable for children. There is even a shorter variant available if you prefer a less strenuous option for younger hikers.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other hiking routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. You'll find sections that overlap with the Andalucía - North Route (Província de Jaén) and the Camino Mozárabe, as well as local routes like Calle Rosalía de Castro and Calle Teresa de Calcuta.

faqs.question: Are there any viewpoints with panoramic scenery?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. The trail is renowned for its breathtaking panoramic views. You can expect vistas of Alcalá la Real, the historic Fortaleza de la Mota, and vast olive groves. On clear days, you might even see the distant peaks of Sierra Nevada and Sierra Sur.

faqs.question: What is the typical duration for completing the Los Zumaques Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is approximately 9.4 to 10 kilometers (around 6 miles) long and typically takes about 3 to 3.5 hours to complete, depending on your pace and stops.

waypoints.name: Fortaleza de la Mota (Castle of Alcalá la Real)
